parsed_review.summary: CP00-260 is a descriptor-only closeout pack that closes the RP07.P06 permission tail (M06 Synthetic Fixture Set tail through M10 Closeout And Next Handoff) and opens the RP07.P07 failure/recovery foundation (M00-M02 head) as eight descriptor sections over 150 planned RP07 units (RP07.P06.M06.S14-RP07.P07.M02.S12). All distributions reconcile across plan, manifest, baseline, and registry requirements: phase counts (RP07.P06=98 + RP07.P07=52 = 150), deliverable counts (claude_review 6, ui 17, security_audit 28, test 22, implementation 35, hermes_evidence 5, failure_recovery 35, fixture 2 = 150), and micro-title counts (sum 150). All 7 required public exports exist in service.js/validators.js, are surfaced through the wildcard packages/search/src/index.js barrel (unchanged but correct), are imported/asserted in model.test.js, and are deep-equal projected into contracts/search-core-contract.json. No-write/no-real-data/descriptor-only boundaries are enforced consistently in freezeCp260Result (all runtime/write/leak flags false, synthetic_only/no_real_data/deny_over_allow_enforced true), SEARCH_CORE_CP260_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (promotes_claude_to_final_approval false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation false), the descriptor validator, and unit tests. Authority boundary holds: Claude is read-only with tools Read/Grep/Glob and is not the final approver (claude_is_final_approval false; human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening true); enterprise trust is not claimed from local validation. Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor evidence/receipt shape (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt false; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product false), not product runtime. Command evidence records all 19 implementation/plan/repo-wide gates passing (exit 0), with pack-level production_ready honestly false (status implemented_pending_review) and closeout-pack:validate correctly deferred as a post-review gate; post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are appropriately not yet produced. Review-hardening preconditions are clean: auth preflight passed with well-formed JSON, no in-scope invalid review attempts, and the unrelated LDIP invalid-attempt file is explicitly preserved out of scope. One non-blocking P3 defense-in-depth observation on validator symmetry is noted. No P0/P1/P2 issues; the pack's gating posture and boundaries are sound.

findings.title: Failure-section descriptor validator deep-checks RP07.P07.M01 rows but not the identical RP07.P07.M00 rows
findings.details: In validateSearchCoreCp260P06CloseoutP07FailureFoundationDescriptor, the shared failSection loop (lines 5201-5208) asserts cross_tenant/permission_denied/ambiguous_rule/lock_conflict/retry_exhaustion guards for all three failure micros (RP07.P07.M00, M01, M02). However, the extra row-specific guards for rollback_expectation.performs_rollback_runtime, blocked_claim_receipt.blocked_claim_detail_included, failure_fixture.real_client_data_loaded, failure_integration_smoke.dispatches_integration_smoke_runtime, audit_failure_hint.audit_hint_detail_included, and hermes_failure_evidence.emits_hermes_runtime_receipt (lines 5209-5215) are asserted only against the M01 section (const m01 = sections['RP07.P07.M01']). RP07.P07.M00 contains the same six row titles (per required_section_rows) but is not subjected to these targeted assertions. This is not an exploitable gap: every row in every section (including M00) is still validated descriptor_only===true, runtime_execution===false, customer_safe_errors_only===true, and raw_payload_included===false by the universal loop (lines 5181-5200), and all rows are deterministically generated from the frozen SEARCH_CORE_CP260_ROW_EXTRAS, so no divergent/unsafe value can be present in M00. The validator and full test/command-evidence suite pass (exit 0). It is purely a validator-symmetry / defense-in-depth observation.
findings.recommendation: For future descriptor packs, extend the M01-specific deep assertions to also cover RP07.P07.M00 (which carries the same rollback/blocked-claim/fixture/smoke/audit-hint/hermes row titles), e.g. by iterating the targeted checks over both M00 and M01. No change is required for CP00-260 closeout, since the universal descriptor-only loop and frozen, deterministic row extras already guarantee the boundary on every M00 row.
findings.post_review_disposition: non_blocking_defense_in_depth_suggestion; does not block pack or goal closeout; logged for future pack hardening
